skill.name: amazon-reviews-api-skill
skill.description: Extract Amazon product reviews using the BrowserAct Amazon Reviews API. Provide an ASIN to get structured review data including ratings, review text, verified purchase status, and reviewer profiles without building crawlers or requiring an Amazon account.
skill.summary: Automated Amazon product review extraction via BrowserAct API — no captchas, no IP blocks, no hallucinations.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 49 patterns across 2 files (186 lines). After semantic review, all flagged patterns are either legitimate API wrapper behavior or false positives from markdown documentation. Network requests go exclusively to the documented BrowserAct API endpoint (api.browseract.com). Environment variable access is limited to BROWSERACT_API_KEY for API authentication. External command patterns in SKILL.md are markdown code blocks, not actual shell execution. No obfuscation, no data exfiltration, no suspicious endpoints detected. The critical heuristic combining code execution, network, and credential access was dismissed as the expected and documented behavior of this API wrapper skill.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: The Python script makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com for workflow execution, status polling, and result retrieval. These are documented, legitimate API calls to the BrowserAct service that the skill is designed to wrap. All URLs are hardcoded to the official BrowserAct domain.

low_findings.title: API Key via Environment Variable
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables using os.getenv() for Bearer token authentication. This is standard practice for API key management. The key is transmitted only to api.browseract.com and never logged or exfiltrated elsewhere.

low_findings.title: Python Script Execution via Shell
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md instructs the agent to execute a Python script via bash command. This is the standard skill execution pattern. The ASIN argument is passed as a JSON payload value, not interpolated into shell commands, eliminating command injection risk.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs to External Site
low_findings.description: SKILL.md and the Python script print URLs pointing users to browseract.com for API key setup. These are informational links in documentation and error messages, not data exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 18 backtick patterns in SKILL.md as shell execution. These are markdown code blocks and inline code formatting in documentation, not actual command execution. All 18 detections across lines 20-64 are false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography False Positives in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md lines 3, 10, 29, 48, and 53. These lines contain documentation text describing product features, ASIN examples, and data output fields. No cryptographic code exists in this file.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance at Python error handling lines (36-37, 42) and SKILL.md feature descriptions (line 13, 63-64). These lines check API response status and describe product capabilities. No reconnaissance behavior exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dangerous Combination Heuristic Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The critical heuristic flagged the combination of code execution, network, and credential access as suspicious. After review, this combination is the expected and documented behavior of an API wrapper skill. The script executes locally, calls a single known API endpoint, and uses credentials only for authentication to that endpoint.

content.user_title: Extract Amazon Product Reviews via API
content.value_statement: Collecting Amazon product reviews manually requires building crawlers, handling captchas, and managing IP restrictions. This skill automates review extraction through BrowserAct's API, delivering structured review data from any Amazon ASIN without an Amazon account or custom scraping infrastructure.
actual_capabilities: Extract structured Amazon product reviews by providing an ASIN
Return reviewer names, ratings, review titles, descriptions, and dates
Identify verified purchases and reviewer countries
Include reviewer profile links for market research
Poll task status with timestamped progress logs
Handle authentication errors with clear user guidance
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key from the user
Task execution may take several minutes for browser automation
Only extracts reviews available on the public Amazon product page
Does not bypass Amazon regional availability restrictions

use_cases.title: Competitor Product Analysis
use_cases.description: Marketing analysts extract competitor product reviews to identify strengths, weaknesses, and customer sentiment trends.

use_cases.title: Customer Feedback Monitoring
use_cases.description: Product managers monitor recent reviews for their own products to detect quality issues and track customer satisfaction.

use_cases.title: Market Research Data Collection
use_cases.description: Researchers collect review data across product categories to analyze customer preferences, common complaints, and purchase patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Single Product Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract Amazon reviews for ASIN B07TS6R1SF.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants reviews for a specific product they already know the ASIN for.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Product Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Get reviews for these Amazon products: B07TS6R1SF, B08N5WRWJ6, and B09XYZ1234. Compare the average ratings and top complaints for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to compare customer feedback across multiple competing products.

prompt_templates.title: Verified Purchase Filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract reviews for ASIN B07TS6R1SF and focus on verified purchase reviews. Summarize what verified buyers say about product quality.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to filter out potentially unreliable unverified reviews for quality assessment.

prompt_templates.title: Sentiment Trend Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all recent reviews for ASIN B08N5WRWJ6 from the past 3 months. Analyze sentiment over time and identify if customer satisfaction is improving or declining. Highlight recurring complaints.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user tracking product performance through longitudinal review analysis.

output_examples.input: Extract Amazon reviews for ASIN B07TS6R1SF.
output_examples.output: I found 10 recent reviews for this product. Average rating: 4.2 out of 5 stars. Key themes: customers praise the build quality and ease of use, while some mention shipping delays. Seven out of 10 reviews are from verified purchasers. The most recent review gave 5 stars and highlighted responsive customer support.

output_examples.input: Get reviews for these Amazon products and compare: B07TS6R1SF, B08N5WRWJ6.
output_examples.output: Comparison complete. Product A (B07TS6R1SF): 4.2 stars from 10 reviews, top complaint is packaging. Product B (B08N5WRWJ6): 3.8 stars from 8 reviews, top complaint is battery life. Product A scores higher on durability, while Product B wins on price value.

output_examples.input: Extract verified purchase reviews for ASIN B09XYZ1234 and summarize quality feedback.
output_examples.output: Found 6 verified purchase reviews with an overall rating of 4.5 out of 5. Verified buyers consistently mention excellent image quality, intuitive controls, and sturdy construction. One verified buyer reported a minor issue with the lens cap fit that was resolved by customer service.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable before using the skill to avoid repeated prompts
Provide the exact ASIN from the Amazon product URL for accurate results
Allow several minutes for task completion and monitor the timestamped status logs for progress
anti_patterns: Do not pass full Amazon URLs instead of ASINs — extract the ASIN from the URL first
Do not retry more than once on failures unless the error specifically mentions invalid authorization
Do not use this skill for products that require an Amazon login to view reviews

faq.question: What is an ASIN and where do I find it?
faq.answer: ASIN stands for Amazon Standard Identification Number. It is a 10-character alphanumeric code found in every Amazon product URL after /dp/ (for example, B07TS6R1SF).

faq.question: Do I need an Amazon account to use this skill?
faq.answer: No Amazon account is required. The skill uses BrowserAct automated browser infrastructure to access public product pages.

faq.question: How long does it take to extract reviews?
faq.answer: Extraction typically takes 1 to 3 minutes. The script outputs timestamped status logs so you can monitor progress in real time.

faq.question: What data fields are included in the results?
faq.answer: Each review includes the reviewer name, profile link, star rating, review title, full review text, publication date, reviewer country, product variant, and verified purchase status.

faq.question: Is there a limit on how many reviews I can extract?
faq.answer: The number of reviews depends on your BrowserAct API plan. Contact BrowserAct for rate limits and usage quotas on your account.

faq.question: What should I do if the extraction fails?
faq.answer: First check if the error mentions invalid authorization, which means your API key needs updating. For other errors, the skill will automatically retry once before reporting the issue to you.
